name: strong_warming_trend_aggressive_setback
description: Strong warming trend: tomorrow +15F above today. setback_modifier=-3.0 passed via classification event. Bedtime fires at 22:30. Setback target = comfort_cool(74) + DEFAULT_SETBACK_DEPTH_COOL_F(3) + setback_modifier(-3) = 74F (floored by logic: modifier is subtracted from comfort-cool direction). Verifies modifier deepens cool setback.
notes: So setback is 74F — same as comfort_cool. This makes sense: a -3 modifier exactly cancels the
default 3F depth, resulting in no net setback depth (cooling all the way to comfort target overnight).
The key assertion is that setback_applied fires with the modifier-adjusted target (74F, NOT 77F).
The modifier is passed in the classification event's setback_modifier field.
Covers Priority 7 warming trend setback from plan abundant-mapping-clarke.md.

events.note: Warm-day classification with strong warming trend modifier (-3.0). Tomorrow forecast +15F above today.
Bedtime: cool setback with modifier. raw=74+3+(-3)=74F. ceiling=79F. target=74F.

assertions.reason: Warm-day classification applied: cool mode at comfort_cool=74F; setback_modifier=-3.0 stored for bedtime
Bedtime cool setback with modifier: comfort_cool(74) + depth(3) + modifier(-3) = 74F. Modifier deepens setback toward comfort target — maximally pre-cools for the hot tomorrow.

verdict.summary: Strong warming trend modifier (-3.0) reduces cool setback target to comfort floor (74F) for aggressive pre-cooling
verdict.expected_behavior: setback_modifier=-3.0 on cool day: bedtime target = comfort_cool+3-3 = comfort_cool (74F); deepest valid pre-cooling setback
